blocks:
Ayahs

ٱقْتَرَبَتِ ٱلسَّاعَةُ وَٱنشَقَّ ٱلْقَمَرُ (۱)
The Hour has drawn near and the moon is split. (1)
وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ ءَايَةً يُعْرِضُوا۟ وَيَقُولُوا۟ سِحْرٌ مُّسْتَمِرٌّ (۲)
If they see a sign, they turn away, and say, ‘An incessant magic!’ (2)
وَكَذَّبُوا۟ وَٱتَّبَعُوٓا۟ أَهْوَآءَهُمْ ۚ وَكُلُّ أَمْرٍ مُّسْتَقِرٌّ (۳)
They denied and followed their own desires, and every matter has a setting [appropriate to it]. (3)

Key Points

The incident of the splitting of the moon (شق القمر) has been discussed in the Shi'a and Sunni commentaries (tafasir) and hadith books. Based on narrations, the suggestion to split the moon came from the disbelievers to the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله because they claimed: magic and sorcery have no effect in the heavens, and if the moon were split, the act of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله would definitely be a miracle. It is interesting that the splitting of the moon occurred in the dead of night and without prior publicity. Travelers between Mecca and Sham (Syria) on the road, and even the people of India, saw the moon split, and it is written in some ancient Indian artifacts that the completion date of that structure was when the moon was split in two. (1) The separation of the planets of the solar system from the sun, or celestial stones from the stars, is an indication of the splitting of the moon (Shaqq al-Qamar) and the possibility of the moon being split. Although two miracles occurred here: one, the splitting, and the other, the return to its original state.

1) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

  1. The Hour (Qiyamah/Resurrection) is near and inevitable, and little remains of the life of this world. Therefore, let us gather provisions for the Hereafter and not be heedless. «اقتربت السّاعة»

  2. The establishment of the Hour is an extremely sensitive and important time. «اقتربت السّاعة» (The Surah speaks of the Hour without introduction, similar to someone who suddenly shouts: Earthquake! Earthquake!).

  3. In conversation with the heedless hard-hearted, warn them about the Hour. «اقتربت السّاعة» (Just as we read at the beginning of Surah Al-Anbiya: «اقتربت النّاس حسابهم و هم فى غفلة»)

  4. The splitting of the moon (Shaqq al-Qamar) is one of the special miracles of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and a sign of the proximity of the Islamic Ummah to the Hour. «اقتربت الساعة وانشق القمر»

  5. The Prophet had many miracles, and the disbelievers considered them all magic. «سحرٌ مستمر»

  6. The method of obstinate individuals when encountering divine miracles and signs is avoidance, denial, and accusation. «ان یروا آیة یعرضوا و یقولوا سحر»

  7. The root of turning away from the truth and denying religion is the following of carnal desires. «یعرضوا... كذبوا... اتّبعوا اهواءهم»

  8. "The end of the dark night is white" (i.e., relief follows hardship). Ultimately, the truth will be established, and the reality of the Resurrection will be proven. «كلّ امر مستقر»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Qamar

This Surah was revealed in Mecca and has 55 verses. Due to the mention of the issue of "Shaqq al-Qamar" (the splitting of the moon) in the first verse, this Surah is named "Al-Qamar" (The Moon). All the verses of this Surah end with the letter "Rāʾ" (ر). The end of the previous Surah was about the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and the beginning of this Surah also speaks of the Resurrection. The content of this Surah mostly relates to the Hour, Prophethood, and the incidents involving the opponents of the prophets. Also, in this Surah, Allah refers to the ominous fate of five groups: the people of Noah, 'Ad, Thamud, Lot, and Pharaoh.

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ جَآءَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَنۢبَآءِ مَا فِيهِ مُزْدَجَرٌ (٤)
There have already come to them reports containing admonishment, (4)
حِكْمَةٌۢ بَـٰلِغَةٌ ۖ فَمَا تُغْنِ ٱلنُّذُرُ (٥)
[and representing] far-reaching wisdom; but warnings are of no avail! (5)
فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ۘ يَوْمَ يَدْعُ ٱلدَّاعِ إِلَىٰ شَىْءٍ نُّكُرٍ (٦)
So turn away from them! The day when the Caller calls to a dire thing, (6)

Key Points

Muzdajir (مزدجر) derives from zajr (زجر), meaning repulsion or prohibition. That is, news about the Day of Judgment that can prevent the commission of sin. Nukur (نُكُر) means something unknown and unpleasant.

Messages

1- Reformers and preachers must be aware of the history of past nations and important events. «جاءهم من الانباء»

2- Familiarity with history can be a means of awareness, awakening, and a deterrent against sin for humanity. «من الانباء ما فیه مُزدَجر»

3- The news of the Day of Judgment in the Quran can be a means of restraining man from disbelief and sin. «اقتربت الساعة... الانباء ما فیه مزدجر»

4- Real historical events and stories are instructive (full of wisdom). «جاءهم من الانباء... حكمة بالغة» The lack of effect of the prophets' religious propagation on people is not a sign of the preacher being poor or the message being weak.

5- Allah completes the argument (establishes proof), «حكمة بالغة», but people ignore the warnings. «فما تغن النذر»

6- If desires and whims dominate a person, neither the direct propagation of the Prophet nor his indirect propagation will be effective. «اتّبعوا اهوائهم... جائهم من الانباء مافیه مزدجر... فماتغن النذر»

7- A wise educator presents his warnings within the context of true stories. «انباء، حكمة، النذر»

8- The messages of the Quran are rational and knowable, not beyond the scope of human comprehension. «حكمة بالغة»

9- Compassion and responsibility have limits, and sometimes turning away (disregard) is necessary. (After conveying wisdom and successive warnings in guidance and education, one must turn away.) «فتولّ عنهم» Sa'di says:

The salty earth yields no hyacinth, Do not waste the seed of action therein.

10- On the Day of Judgment, there will be callers who summon people to the Fire (Hell). «یدع الداع»

11- The scenes of the Day of Judgment will be very unpleasant and unexpected for the deviants and those from whom the Prophet has turned away. «شى ء نُكُر»

Ayah

خُشَّعًا أَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ كَأَنَّهُمْ جَرَادٌ مُّنتَشِرٌ (۷)
they will emerge from the graves as if they were scattered locusts with a humbled look [in their eyes], (7)

Key Points

Ajdaath (أجداث) is the plural of jadath (جَدَث), meaning grave. The lowering of the gaze is either due to intense fear or intense shame, which leads to humiliation and disgrace. The comparison of the criminals on the Day of Judgment to scattered locusts indicates their confusion and bewilderment when exiting their graves.

Messages

1- The spiritual and psychological states of a person affect his body and appearance. «خشّعاً ابصارهم»

2- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical (bodily). «یخرجون من الاجداث»

3- One of the methods of education is the use of examples and analogies. «كانهم جراد منتشر» The best examples are the most tangible ones.

Ayah

مُّهْطِعِينَ إِلَى ٱلدَّاعِ ۖ يَقُولُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ هَـٰذَا يَوْمٌ عَسِرٌ (۸)
scrambling toward the summoner. The faithless will say, ‘This is a hard day!’ (8)

Key Points

The term مُهطعین (muhṭi'īn) is derived from اهطاع (ihṭā‘), meaning to raise the head upwards out of anxiety, or to stare fixedly, or to run quickly. Disbelievers will face various types of hardships on the Day of Resurrection, including: the hardship of helplessness, the hardship of the long duration (of the Day), the hardship of thirst and hunger, the hardship of wicked neighbors, the hardship of disgrace, the hardship of regrets, the hardship of accountability (reckoning), the hardship of humiliation, the hardship of lacking an intercessor, the hardship of separation from the righteous, the hardship of lacking provisions (for the journey), and the hardship of the complaints of others. While for the people of faith, on that Day, there will be neither fear nor terror concerning the past, nor grief nor dread concerning the future. لا خوف علیهم ولا هم یحزنون (There shall be no fear upon them, nor shall they grieve).

Messages

1- The haste of people in emerging from their graves is to answer the caller of the Day of Resurrection. مهطعین الى الدّاع (hastening toward the Caller).

2- Presence in the arena of Resurrection will be through the call and invitation of callers. الى الدّاع (toward the Caller).

3- The Day of Resurrection is difficult for the disbelievers, not for the believers. یقول الكافرون هذا یوم عسر (The disbelievers will say, "This is a distressful day").

Ayahs

۞ كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ فَكَذَّبُوا۟ عَبْدَنَا وَقَالُوا۟ مَجْنُونٌ وَٱزْدُجِرَ (۹)
The people of Noah impugned before them. So they impugned Our servant and said, ‘A crazy man,’ and he was reviled. (9)
فَدَعَا رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّى مَغْلُوبٌ فَٱنتَصِرْ (۱٠)
Thereat he invoked his Lord, [saying,] ‘I have been overcome, so help [me].’ (10)
فَفَتَحْنَآ أَبْوَٰبَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ بِمَآءٍ مُّنْهَمِرٍ (۱۱)
Then We opened the gates of the sky with pouring waters, (11)

Key Points

The word «fantasir» (فانتصر) is used instead of «fansur» (فانصر), meaning: O Allah! Help me for the sake of Your religion; my victory is the victory of Your path. (2) «Munhamir» (منهمر) derives from «hamr», meaning the pouring down of tears or water. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Prophet Nuh (Noah) invited people to the straight path openly and secretly for nine hundred and fifty years, but when he saw them rebellious, he raised his hands in supplication and said: «Rabbi innī maghlūb» (My Lord, indeed I am overcome). (3) The word maghlūb (مغلوب) is used only once in the entire Qur'an, and it is concerning Prophet Nuh. Similarly, among the divine salutations upon the prophets, only one salutation includes the phrase «fī al-‘ālamīn» (among the worlds), and that too is concerning Prophet Nuh عليه السلام: «Salāmun ‘alā Nūḥin fī al-‘ālamīn» (Peace be upon Noah among the worlds). (4)

(2) Mufradāt Rāghib. (3) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. (4) Saffat, 79.

Messages

1- Allah consoles the Prophet of Islam, saying: Do not worry; other prophets also faced your difficulties. «Kadhdhabat qablahum qawmu Nūḥ» (The people of Noah denied before them).

2- Studying the history of those who passed away is a source of encouragement for patience and steadfastness. «Kadhdhabat qablahum» (denied before them).

3- The beginning of the divine discourse concerning the peoples of Nuh, ‘Ad, Thamud, and Lut with the phrase «kadhdhabat» (denied) indicates the danger of denial (rejection). «Kadhdhabat qablahum qawmu Nūḥ...» and «Kadhdhabat ‘Ād... Kadhdhabat Thamūd... Kadhdhabat qawmu Lūṭ» which will appear in the subsequent verses.

4- The most important characteristic of the prophets was servitude and worship of Allah. «‘abdanā» (Our servant).

5- Wrongdoers justify their actions by slandering and defaming others. «Kadhdhabū ‘abdanā wa qālū majnūnun wa uzdujir» (They denied Our servant and said, "A madman," and he was repelled).

6- Prophets are hopeful of divine grace even at the height of distress. «Fa da‘ā Rabbah» (So he called upon his Lord).

7- Being overcome or weak is not a sign of falsehood. «innī maghlūb...» (indeed I am overcome...).

8- The supplications of the prophets are answered. «Fa da‘ā... fa fataḥnā abwāba al-samā’» (So he called... then We opened the gates of the heaven).

9- Supplication and cutting off hope from people are the prerequisites for deliverance and the answering of prayer. «innī maghlūb... fa fataḥnā abwāba al-samā’» (indeed I am overcome... then We opened the gates of the heaven).

10- Natural elements are like divine helpers. «bi mā’in munhamir» (with water pouring forth).

11- Manifestations of mercy sometimes become means of punishment. «bi mā’in munhamir» (with water pouring forth).

Ayahs

وَفَجَّرْنَا ٱلْأَرْضَ عُيُونًا فَٱلْتَقَى ٱلْمَآءُ عَلَىٰٓ أَمْرٍ قَدْ قُدِرَ (۱۲)
and We made the earth burst forth with springs, and the waters met for a preordained purpose. (12)
وَحَمَلْنَـٰهُ عَلَىٰ ذَاتِ أَلْوَٰحٍ وَدُسُرٍ (۱۳)
We bore him on a vessel made of planks and nails, (13)
تَجْرِى بِأَعْيُنِنَا جَزَآءً لِّمَن كَانَ كُفِرَ (۱٤)
which sailed [over the flood waters] in Our sight, as a retribution for him who was met with disbelief. (14)
وَلَقَد تَّرَكْنَـٰهَآ ءَايَةً فَهَلْ مِن مُّدَّكِرٍ (۱٥)
Certainly We have left it as a sign; so is there anyone who will be admonished? (15)
فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عَذَابِى وَنُذُرِ (۱٦)
So how were My punishment and warnings? (16)

Key Points

Dusr (دُسُر) is the plural of disār (دسار), meaning nail (or peg). Nudhur (نُذُر) is the plural of nadhīr (نذیر), but in such contexts, it means warning, not the warner. (5) Noah's Ark possessed certain characteristics, including: a) Its construction was by divine command. «اصنع الفلك» b) Its builder was the Prophet of Allah. c) The location of the ark's construction was in a dry desert, far from the sea, and according to narrations, it was the Mosque of Kufa, which led to the mockery of the disbelievers. d) Male and female animals of every species were loaded onto it. e) Its movement was under the supervision of Allah. «تجرى باعیننا» f) Waves like mountains were in its path. «فى موج كالجبال» (6) g) All the believers on Earth boarded it. h) It was the sole means of safety and salvation. Despite all these characteristics mentioned for Noah's Ark, Allah refers to it using «الواح و دُسر» (planks and nails) instead of the word safīnah (ship) or ark, meaning just a collection of planks and nails. Perhaps this minimization is so that Noah's salvation is attributed to His will, not the grandeur of the ship. In Ayah 15, the planks and nails, which were the means of salvation for a Prophet, are introduced as a divine sign (āyah). Are the graves of those who themselves were the cause of salvation not divine signs? «ذات الواح و دسر... تركناها آیة...»

5) Lisān al-Arab. 6) Hūd, 42.

Messages

1- The gushing of springs and the flow of waters are by the divine will. «و فجّرنا الارض عیوناً»

2- As soon as the will of Allah is attached to a matter, the heavens and the earth cooperate. «فالتقى الماء»

3- Divine wrath and the coordination of natural factors in the heavens and the earth for punishment are calculated and measured. «امر قد قُدر»

4- If we become sincere servants of Allah, He provides everything for us. «عبدنا - فتحنا - فجّرنا - حملنا»

5- The course of divine actions is through material means and human efforts. «حملناه على ذات الواح و دسر» Planks and nails are abundant, but it is the will of Allah that makes these planks and nails the means of salvation for His Prophet and the believers.

6- Not only the construction of Noah's Ark, but also its path of movement was under the supervision of Allah. (In another place He says: «اصنع الفلك باعیننا» (7) and here He says: «تجرى باعیننا»)

7- Prophets are a blessing that should not be denied; if they are denied, Allah punishes the disbelievers and rewards the Prophets. «جزاء لمن كان كفر»

8- Not all punishments occur on the Day of Judgment. «جزاء لمن كان كفر»

9- Noah's Ark remains throughout history. «تركناها آیة» (Just as the body of Pharaoh is a sign throughout history. «فالیوم ننجّیك ببدنك لتكون لمن خلفك آیة» (8))

10- Preserving historical relics that recall the factors of a nation's progress or downfall is necessary. «تركناها آیة»

11- A work carried out by divine will and by the hands of the friends of Allah is blessed. (Noah's Ark saved the believers at that time, preserved the lineage of all animals, and became a lesson and a memorial for history.) «تركناها آیة»

12- One must look at the divine signs with precision and insight. «تركناها آیة فهل من مدّكر»

13- For human guidance, external factors alone are not sufficient; the capacity and spirit of accepting advice are necessary. «فهل من مدّكر»

14- The punishment of past nations must serve as a warning for future generations. «عذابى و نذر»

15- Punishment and the wrath of Allah occur after multiple warnings. «عذابى و نذر»

7) Hūd, 37.

8) Yūnus, 92.

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِن مُّدَّكِرٍ (۱۷)
Certainly We have made the Quran simple for the sake of admonishment. So is there anyone who will be admonished? (17)

Key Points

The Quran is easy from various dimensions: in terms of its wording, which makes its recitation easy and beautiful at the same time; in terms of its content, which includes many stories and examples; and also in its conformity with human nature, which makes accepting its teachings easy. This verse is repeated four times in this Surah: in verses 17, 22, 32, and 40. Although the Quran is easy, "یسّرنا القرآن" (We have made the Quran easy), its like cannot be produced, even if all creation joins hands. "لئن اجتمعت الانس والجن..." (9)

9) Al-Isra, 88.

Messages

1- The duty of guides is to speak easily/simply. "یسّرنا"

2- The Quran is easy, but it is not weak. (We read a few verses earlier that the verses of the Quran are "حكمة بالغة," meaning firm and strong words.) "یسّرنا القرآن"

3- The mission of the Quran is awakening and remembrance. "القرآن للذكر"

4- The simpler we speak, the more audience we will have. "یسّرنا... فهل من مدّكر"

5- One must be mindful both regarding the cosmic signs (آیات تكوینى) — "تركناها آیة فهل من مدّكر" (We left it as a sign, so is there any who will remember?) — and regarding the legislative signs (آیات تشریعى). "یسّرنا القرآن للذكر فهل من مُدّكر"

6- Not everyone is worthy of benefiting from the Quran; benefiting is specific to the people of remembrance (اهل ذكر), not the people of heedlessness (اهل غفلت). "فهل من مُدّكر"

7- The existence of a suitable context is not always a condition for acceptance. Sometimes all conditions are met, but man does not remember due to obstinacy and following desires. "فهل من مُدّكر"

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ عَادٌ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عَذَابِى وَنُذُرِ (۱۸)
[The people of] ‘Ad impugned [their apostle]. So how were My punishment and warnings? (18)
إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحًا صَرْصَرًا فِى يَوْمِ نَحْسٍ مُّسْتَمِرٍّ (۱۹)
Indeed We unleashed upon them an icy gale on an incessantly ill-fated day, (19)
تَنزِعُ ٱلنَّاسَ كَأَنَّهُمْ أَعْجَازُ نَخْلٍ مُّنقَعِرٍ (۲٠)
knocking down people as if they were trunks of uprooted palm trees. (20)
فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عَذَابِى وَنُذُرِ (۲۱)
So how were My punishment and warnings?! (21)
وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِن مُّدَّكِرٍ (۲۲)
Certainly We have made the Quran simple for the sake of admonishment. So is there anyone who will be admonished? (22)

Key Points

The meaning of «یوم نحسٍ مستمر» (a continuous day of misfortune) is that this tempest lasted for several consecutive days, just as [Allah] states in Surah Fussilat, Ayah 16: "We sent the wind upon them during several ominous days." Thus, the meaning of mustamirr (مستمر) in this Ayah is continuous and successive days. In Ayah 7 of Surah Al-Haqqah, these ominous days are described as seven nights and eight days: «سبع لیالٍ و ثمانیة ایام» (seven nights and eight days). «منقعر» (munqa'ir) is derived from qa'r, meaning uprooted. «أعجاز» (a'jāz) is the plural of 'ajuz, meaning the rear or lower part, or the trunk (of a tree). The badness or goodness of a time is either due to the good or bad events that occur within it, or there is an inherent misfortune (nahs) or blessing (khayr) in the essence of time itself, of which we are unaware.

Messages

1- The people of 'Ad did not learn a lesson from the destruction of the people of Noah. «كذبت قبلهم قوم نوح... كذبت عاد»

2- The characteristic of the people of 'Ad was denial and rejection. «كذّبت عاد»

3- There is a relationship between warning (admonition) and punishment (First the warning, and if it is ineffective, then the punishment). «فكیف كان عذابى و نذر»

4- Reflect upon the history and destiny of nations. «فكیف كان...»

5- The result of denial is Divine wrath. «كذّبت... عذابى»

6- Natural factors, such as wind, can be a means of punishing people. «كذّبت عاد... ریحاً صرصرا»

7- Times are not all the same. (Some times are blessed, like the Night of Qadr, «لیلة مباركة», and some are ominous and unfortunate.) «یوم نحسٍ»

8- By the will of Allah, wind can be an agent of growth, «ارسلنا الریاح لواقح»(10), and it can also be an agent of destruction (uprooting). «تنزع الناس كانهم اعجاز نخل منقعر»

9- Humanity, whoever they may be, is nothing more than straw and debris before Divine wrath. «تنزع الناس كانّهم اعجاز نخل...»

10- The people of 'Ad were strong and robust people who were uprooted from their foundations. «كانّهم اعجاز نخل»

11- In education (or moral training), repeating certain points is necessary. The phrase «فكیف كان عذابى و نذر» has been repeated.

12- When recounting history, the main goal (taking a lesson) should not be forgotten. (After stating each section, the main point is repeated.) «لقد یسّرنا القرآن للذّكر فهل من مدّكرٍ»

(10) Hijr, 22.

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ بِٱلنُّذُرِ (۲۳)
[The people of] Thamud denied the warnings, (23)
فَقَالُوٓا۟ أَبَشَرًا مِّنَّا وَٰحِدًا نَّتَّبِعُهُۥٓ إِنَّآ إِذًا لَّفِى ضَلَـٰلٍ وَسُعُرٍ (۲٤)
and they said, ‘Are we to follow a lone human from ourselves?! Indeed then we would be in error and madness.’ (24)
أَءُلْقِىَ ٱلذِّكْرُ عَلَيْهِ مِنۢ بَيْنِنَا بَلْ هُوَ كَذَّابٌ أَشِرٌ (۲٥)
‘Has the Reminder been cast upon him from among us? No, he is a self-conceited liar.’ (25)
سَيَعْلَمُونَ غَدًا مَّنِ ٱلْكَذَّابُ ٱلْأَشِرُ (۲٦)
‘Tomorrow they will know who is a self-conceited liar. (26)
إِنَّا مُرْسِلُوا۟ ٱلنَّاقَةِ فِتْنَةً لَّهُمْ فَٱرْتَقِبْهُمْ وَٱصْطَبِرْ (۲۷)
We are sending the She-camel as a trial for them; so watch them and be steadfast. (27)

Key Points

"Su'ur" (سُعُر) is both the plural of "Sa'ir" (سعیر), meaning blazing fire, and the plural of "Sa'r" (سَعْر), meaning madness or insanity. "Ashir" (اَشِر) refers to someone who is infatuated with himself (self-obsessed). In Ayah 24, the disbelievers say to the prophets: If we follow you, we will be in error and the Fire (Hell) or madness! But in Ayah 47, we read: «انّ المجرمین فى ضلال و سُعُر» (Indeed, the criminals are in error and the Fire). The transgressors are in error and the Fire. The people of Thamud asked Prophet Salih to bring forth a she-camel from the heart of the mountain as a miracle, and he did so, but the people killed this Divine she-camel. The disbelievers had several excuses against the prophets, saying: a) He is a human being like us. b) He is one person from among us. c) Our number is large, and it is not appropriate for a multitude to follow an individual who is just like us. «ابشراً منّا واحداً نتبعه» (Shall we follow a human being, one of us?) However, none of these excuses are logical, because: Firstly: Being human is a point of strength for the prophets so that they can be models for other human beings. Secondly: All prophets were individuals, and throughout history, there were many individuals who rose up alone and presented reformative plans. Thirdly: The principle is following the truth, not the number of followers or leaders.

Messages

1- The action of the people of Thamud was continuous denial. «كذبت ثمود بالنذر»

2- Disbelievers lack logic and reasoning when confronting the prophets. They say: Why did he become a prophet and not us? «أبشراً منّا...»

3- The lives of the prophets were ordinary and lived among the people. «أبشراً منّا»

4- Sometimes a person falls so low that he does not accept an infallible individual as a prophet, «أبشر منّا...» but considers an unconscious inanimate object to be God and worships it.

5- Sometimes a person reaches a point where he considers following the infallible Prophet, who possesses logic and miracles, to be deviation. «انا اذاً لفى ضلال و سُعُر»

6- Creating doubt and suspicion among the people is the ground for denying the truth. «كذّبت... ابشراً منا... ءَاُلقى الذكر علیه»

7- Both the Quran is a Reminder (Dhikr), «نحن نزلنا الذكر...»(11) and that which was revealed to previous prophets is also a Reminder. «ءَاُلقى الذكر علیه من بیننا»

8- Obstinate disbelievers called the Prophet of Allah self-obsessed. «كذّاب اشر»

9- Even obstinate disbelievers consider lying and self-obsession to be bad. (And for this reason, they attributed lying and self-obsession to the Prophet). «بل هو كذّاب اشر»

10- Religious preachers must be prepared to hear the harshest accusations. «بل هو كذّاب اشر»

11- Respond to vain talk. «فقالوا... سیعلمون...»

12- The Day of Judgment is the day when truths will be revealed and disbelievers will be disgraced. «سیعلمون غداً من الكذّاب الاشر»

13- Miracles are a means of testing people. «مرسلوا الناقة فتنة لهم»

14- Prophets keep their communities under observation. «فارتقبهم»

15- Religious leaders must be patient. «و اصطبر»

11) Hijr, 9.

Ayahs

وَنَبِّئْهُمْ أَنَّ ٱلْمَآءَ قِسْمَةٌۢ بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ كُلُّ شِرْبٍ مُّحْتَضَرٌ (۲۸)
Inform them that the water is to be shared between them; each of them showing up at his turn.’ (28)
فَنَادَوْا۟ صَاحِبَهُمْ فَتَعَاطَىٰ فَعَقَرَ (۲۹)
But they called their companion, and he took [a knife] and hamstrung [her]. (29)
فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عَذَابِى وَنُذُرِ (۳٠)
So how were My punishment and My warnings?! (30)
إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ صَيْحَةً وَٰحِدَةً فَكَانُوا۟ كَهَشِيمِ ٱلْمُحْتَظِرِ (۳۱)
We sent against them a single Cry, and they became like the dry sticks of a corral builder. (31)
وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِن مُّدَّكِرٍ (۳۲)
Certainly We have made the Quran simple for the sake of admonishment. So is there anyone who will be admonished? (32)

Key Points

The division of water between the people and the camel was an important Divine test, requiring the people to observe this law and only approach the water on the day they were entitled to benefit from it. In Ayah 155 of Surah Ash-Shu'ara, we read that Prophet Salih said: One day the water of the village is for the she-camel and one day is for you. «لها شِربٌ و لكم شرب یوم معلوم» Sharb (شِرب) means portion or share, which is used specifically for the share of water. Ta'ati (تعاطى) means taking something or performing an action that is inappropriate and requires audacity. Since the word Ta'ati (تعاطى) is in the Tafa'ul (mutual action) form, it suggests that an exchange or understanding took place between the people of Thamud and the camel's killer. Aqr (عقر) means hamstringing or bringing down the she-camel. Hashim (هشیم) means dry, crushed vegetation. Muhtathir (مُحتظِر) is someone who gathers dry vegetation for his livestock. Although the killer of the she-camel was only one person, in other verses of the Quran, the act is attributed to all the people, «فعَقروها»(12) or «فعقروا النّاقة»(13). According to narrations, this is because of the consensus and satisfaction of the others regarding the killer's action, and this sin is also counted against them.(14)

12) شعراء،157؛ شمس،14. 13) اعراف، 77. 14) وسائل، ج 11، ص 409.

Messages

1- Sometimes the test involves water rationing, which is rationed by the Prophet's command. «انّ الماء قسمة»

2- If unworthy individuals cannot carry out an action themselves, they call upon those who have the audacity to commit corruption. «فنادَوا صاحبهم»

3- Violating sanctity requires audacity. «صاحبهم فتعاطى»

4- Wicked individuals are ready to cause mischief and do not waste opportunities. «فنادوا... فعقر»

5- If we do not appreciate a blessing that was sent, «مرسلوا الناقة» punishment will be sent. «ارسلنا علیهم صیحةً»

6- In the Quran's recounting of historical events, the time, place, and person are not important; the motive and the result are what matter. (In these verses, neither the name of the person who killed the she-camel nor the time and place of the incident is specified).

7- Although the killer was one person, due to the satisfaction of others with his action, everyone was destroyed. «عقر... ارسلنا علیهم صیحةً»

8- By the will of Allah, an entire nation can be wiped out by a single blast (cry). «صیحة واحدة»

9- Sometimes an entire nation is destroyed because of a single act of disobedience and audacity. «فكانوا كهشیم المحتظِر»

10- Divine wrath turns strong and giant individuals into crushed vegetation. «كهشیم المحتظر»

11- The Quran easily provides the best and most accurate histories and lessons for everyone. «و لقد یسّرنا»

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ لُوطٍۭ بِٱلنُّذُرِ (۳۳)
And the people of Lot denied the warnings. (33)
إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ حَاصِبًا إِلَّآ ءَالَ لُوطٍ ۖ نَّجَّيْنَـٰهُم بِسَحَرٍ (۳٤)
We unleashed upon them a rain of stones, excepting the family of Lot, whom We delivered at dawn (34)
نِّعْمَةً مِّنْ عِندِنَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى مَن شَكَرَ (۳٥)
as a blessing from Us. Thus do We reward those who give thanks. (35)
وَلَقَدْ أَنذَرَهُم بَطْشَتَنَا فَتَمَارَوْا۟ بِٱلنُّذُرِ (۳٦)
He had already warned them of Our punishment, but they disputed the warnings. (36)
وَلَقَدْ رَٰوَدُوهُ عَن ضَيْفِهِۦ فَطَمَسْنَآ أَعْيُنَهُمْ فَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابِى وَنُذُرِ (۳۷)
They even solicited of him his guests, whereat We blotted out their eyes, [saying,] ‘Taste My punishment and My warnings!’ (37)
وَلَقَدْ صَبَّحَهُم بُكْرَةً عَذَابٌ مُّسْتَقِرٌّ (۳۸)
Early at dawn there visited them an abiding punishment: (38)
فَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابِى وَنُذُرِ (۳۹)
‘Taste My punishment and warnings!’ (39)
وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِن مُّدَّكِرٍ (٤٠)
Certainly We have made the Quran simple for the sake of admonishment. So is there anyone who will be admonished? (40)

Key Points

Nudhur (نُذُر) is the plural of Nadhir (نذیر), meaning both 'warner' and 'warning'. Hasib (حاصب) means a strong wind that moves pebbles and sand. Batsh (بطش) means seizing with force and power. Tams (طمس) means obliterating and destroying, and Tamāraw (تماروا) means engaging in dispute. It seems that those who intended harm to the guests of Prophet Lot (عليه السلام) were first blinded and and then destroyed when the region was overturned. As Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: As soon as the people of Lot entered his house, Gabriel blinded them with a gesture.(15) In the story of the people of Lot, the word Nudhur (نُذُر) is mentioned four times and the word ‘Adhāb (عذاب) three times, which indicates the importance of the warning regarding the actions of the people of Lot.

15) كافى،ج ۸، ص ۳۲۹.

Messages

1- It is as if Allah is telling the Prophet: Do not worry, for the Prophets of God have always been denied by the unworthy. «كذّبت قوم لوط»

2- Although the people of ‘Ad, Thamud, Lot, and Noah each had specific characteristics, they were all united in denying their Prophet. «كذّبت... كذّبت... كذّبت»

3- Denying one Prophet is equivalent to denying all the Prophets. «كذّبت... بالنذر»

4- Allah’s hand is open for various types of punishments. «بماء منهمر - ریحاً صرصراً صیحة واحدة - حاصبا»

5- In multiple instances, Allah saves the believers. «نجیناهم بسحرٍ»

6- When punishment descends, the innocent and the guilty do not burn together. «أرسلنا علیهم حاصبا الا آل لوط نجّیناهم بسحر»

7- Blessings are not only material matters; salvation from punishment is among the greatest blessings. «نعمة من عندنا»

8- Faith in the Prophets is gratitude to Allah and a source of salvation. «كذلك نجزى من شكر»

9- Allah first completes the argument (proof) and then punishes. «و لقد انذرهم بطشتنا»

10- Although the mission of the Prophets includes both good tidings and warning, their warning is more frequent due to the people's intoxication (with worldly life) and heedlessness. «انذرهم»

11- Creating doubt about the truth is equivalent to denying it. «كذبت قوم لوط... فتماروا بالنذر»

12- Pollution and corruption know no bounds. (Even though the people of Lot committed immoral acts in public gatherings and saw no impediment for themselves, they still did not leave the guests in the Prophet's house alone.) «راودوه عن ضیفه»

13- As soon as the crime became severe, respite was not permissible. «راودوه عن ضیفه فطمسنا»

14- May the eye that looks with evil intent upon innocent guests, especially in the house of the Prophet, be blinded. «فطمسنا اعینهم»

15- Divine wrath and punishment cannot be neutralized. «عذاب مستقّر»

16- One of the educational methods of the Quran is the narration of instructive stories. «و لقد یسّرنا القرآن»

17- Reading the Quran is not enough; one must take heed. «فهل من مدّكر»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ جَآءَ ءَالَ فِرْعَوْنَ ٱلنُّذُرُ (٤۱)
Certainly the warnings did come to Pharaoh’s clan (41)
كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا كُلِّهَا فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُمْ أَخْذَ عَزِيزٍ مُّقْتَدِرٍ (٤۲)
who denied all of Our signs. So We seized them with the seizing of One [who is] all-mighty, Omnipotent. (42)
أَكُفَّارُكُمْ خَيْرٌ مِّنْ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكُمْ أَمْ لَكُم بَرَآءَةٌ فِى ٱلزُّبُرِ (٤۳)
Are your faithless better than those? Have you [been granted] [some sort of] immunity in the scriptures? (43)
أَمْ يَقُولُونَ نَحْنُ جَمِيعٌ مُّنتَصِرٌ (٤٤)
Do they say, ‘We are a confederate league’? (44)
سَيُهْزَمُ ٱلْجَمْعُ وَيُوَلُّونَ ٱلدُّبُرَ (٤٥)
The league will be routed and turn its back [to flee]. (45)
بَلِ ٱلسَّاعَةُ مَوْعِدُهُمْ وَٱلسَّاعَةُ أَدْهَىٰ وَأَمَرُّ (٤٦)
Indeed, the Hour is their tryst; and the Hour will be most calamitous and bitter. (46)

Key Points

Sayuhzam (سیهزم) is derived from hazm (هزم), meaning to press down upon a dry object in such a way that it disintegrates. Adhā (أَدْهى) means a greater calamity. Regarding the peoples of Noah, 'Ad, Thamud, and Lot, the issue was only the denial of warnings (an-nudhur), but concerning the Pharaoh's people (Fir'awn), the denial of signs (āyāt) and miracles was also involved. This is the fifth and final group of disbelievers mentioned in this Surah. The factors leading to arrogance are several: A) When a person feels that he possesses a special perfection or value and is better than others. (A-kuffārukum khayr). B) When he imagines that he has a guarantee of safety from Allah and is immune from punishment. (Am lakum barā'ah). C) When he relies on his companions and like-minded individuals. (Am yaqūlūna naḥnu jamī'un muntaṣir).

Messages

1- Whoever is like-minded or obedient to someone is considered part of them. (Āl Fir'awn).

2- The social system during the time of Pharaoh was individual-centric and tyrannical, and everyone was attributed to one person (Āl Fir'awn), whereas among other nations, a tribal and communal system prevailed. (Qawm Nūḥ... Qawm Lūṭ).

3- Allah completes the proof (Hujjah) against people. (Jā'a... an-nudhur).

4- In the Pharaonic system, the prophets, their miracles, and the divine warnings were all denied. (Kadhabū bi-āyātinā kullahā).

5- The destiny of humanity is in its own hands. (Kadhabū... fa-akhadhnāhum).

6- If a person blocks all paths of truth against himself, divine wrath will descend. (Kadhabū bi-āyātinā kullahā fa-akhadhnāhum).

7- Those who display power, like Pharaoh, must be dealt with powerfully. (Akhdh 'azīzin muqtadir).

8- All powers are subject to decline and defeat; the only impenetrable power is His power. ('Azīzun muqtadir).

9- Divine wrath is accompanied by power and firmness. (Akhdh 'azīzin muqtadir).

10- Divine wrath is based on disbelief, obstinacy, and denial, and the disbelievers of every age have no advantage over their predecessors. (A-kuffārukum khayr...).

11- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), argue in such a way that you take away all the enemy's avenues and hypotheses. (A-kuffārukum khayr... Am lakum barā'ah... Am yaqūlūna naḥnu jamī'un muntaṣir).

12- In the Quran's method of guidance, history, threat, argumentation, and belief are intertwined. History: (Jā'a Āl Fir'awn...), Threat: (Akhdh 'azīz... Sayuhzamu al-jam'u...), Argumentation: (A-kuffārukum... Am... Am...), Belief: (As-Sā'atu maw'iduhum wa-as-Sā'atu adhā wa-amarr).

13- Break the enemy's awe; disbelievers only consider material standards and are heedless of unseen and divine powers. (Naḥnu jamī'un muntaṣir... Sayuhzamu al-jam'u).

14- Deal decisively with the arrogant enemy. (Sayuhzamu al-jam'u wa-yuwallūna ad-dubur).

15- The element of disbelief and denial is a means of dispersion in any community. (Sayuhzam... Yuwallūna ad-dubur).

16- If the disbelievers boast of their multitude, let them know that they will be scattered (Sayuhzamu al-jam'u); and if they rely on mutual support, let them know that they will all flee. (Sayuwallūna ad-dubur).

17- For the disbelievers, in addition to worldly destruction, there is also the wrath of the Hereafter awaiting them. (Bal as-Sā'atu maw'iduhum).

18- The severest worldly calamities are nothing compared to the calamities and bitterness of the Hereafter. (Wa-as-Sā'atu adhā wa-amarr).

19- The worldly defeat of the disbelievers is a prelude to the harsher punishment of the Hereafter. (Sayuhzamu al-jam'u... As-Sā'atu adhā wa-amarr).

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ وَسُعُرٍ (٤۷)
Indeed the guilty are steeped in error and madness. (47)
يَوْمَ يُسْحَبُونَ فِى ٱلنَّارِ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمْ ذُوقُوا۟ مَسَّ سَقَرَ (٤۸)
The day when they are dragged on their faces into the Fire, [it will be said to them,] ‘Taste the touch of hell!’ (48)
إِنَّا كُلَّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقْنَـٰهُ بِقَدَرٍ (٤۹)
Indeed We have created everything in a measure, (49)
وَمَآ أَمْرُنَآ إِلَّا وَٰحِدَةٌ كَلَمْحٍۭ بِٱلْبَصَرِ (٥٠)
and Our command is but a single [word], like the twinkling of an eye. (50)

Key Points

"Su'ur" (سُعُر) can be the plural of "Sa'īr" (سعیر) (blazing fire) or the plural of "Sa'ar" (سَعر) (madness/insanity). "Yusḥabūn" (یسحبون) means being dragged, and "Saḥāb" (سحاب) is a cloud that the wind drags. "Saqar" (سقر) means Hellfire, and originally refers to the burning of the skin due to heat. "Lamḥ" (لمح) is the flashing of lightning. In a tradition concerning the verse "Innā kulla shay'in khalaqnāhu bi-qadar" (انّا كل شى ء خلقناه بقدر), we read that Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Allah says: We created everything for the inhabitants of Hellfire commensurate with their deeds. (16)

16) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, criminals will be bewildered, confused, and afflicted by fire. "fī ḍalālin wa su'ur" (فى ضلال و سُعُر) (based on "Su'ur" meaning blazing fire).

2- A person who condemns himself to Hellfire through bad choices is senseless. "fī ḍalālin wa su'ur" (فى ضلال و سُعُر) (based on "Su'ur" meaning madness/insanity).

3- Denying and rejecting the Divine religion is the most prominent crime. "Innal-mujrimīn..." (انّ المجرمین...)

4- The arrogant ones of this world will be the humiliated ones on the Day of Judgment. "Naḥnu jamī'un muntaṣir... yusḥabūna fīn-nāri 'alā wujūhihim" (نحن جمیع منتصر... یسحبون فى النّار على وجوههم)

5- Existence has a precise purpose, reckoning, and system. "Kullu shay'in khalaqnāhu bi-qadar" (كلّ شى ء خلقناه بقدر)

6- Every instance of the emergence of nations, the sending of Prophets, and the punishment and reward of humans occurs within the framework of specific regulations and a system. "Innā kulla shay'in khalaqnāhu bi-qadar" (انّا كلّ شى ء خلقناه بقدرٍ)

7- Divine actions are both calculated and wise, and they are executed swiftly. "bi-qadar... ka-lamḥin bil-baṣar" (بقدر... كلمحٍ بالبصر)

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَهْلَكْنَآ أَشْيَاعَكُمْ فَهَلْ مِن مُّدَّكِرٍ (٥۱)
Certainly We have destroyed your likes. So is there anyone who will be admonished? (51)
وَكُلُّ شَىْءٍ فَعَلُوهُ فِى ٱلزُّبُرِ (٥۲)
Everything they have done is in the books, (52)
وَكُلُّ صَغِيرٍ وَكَبِيرٍ مُّسْتَطَرٌ (٥۳)
and everything big and small is committed to writing. (53)
إِنَّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ وَنَهَرٍ (٥٤)
Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and streams, (54)
فِى مَقْعَدِ صِدْقٍ عِندَ مَلِيكٍ مُّقْتَدِرٍۭ (٥٥)
in the abode of truthfulness with an omnipotent King. (55)

Key Points

Ashyā‘ means counterparts, those of the same thought, followers, and those of the same path. Zubur is the plural of Zabūr, meaning book. And mustatar means written, arranged, and lined up. This Surah began with warning and admonition and concludes with glad tidings.

Messages

1- Allah’s tradition (Sunnah) in punishing disbelievers who share the same ideology is uniform. «أهلكنا أشیاعكم»

2- We must take the Divine threat seriously.

3- Take lessons from history. «فهل من مدّكر»

4- No deed is erased. «فى الزُّبر»

5- The recording of deeds is a source of comfort for the pious, anxiety for the criminals, and a deterrent for man against disbelief and sin. «فى الزبر و كلّ صغیر و كبیر مستطر»

7- The deeds recorded in the Book are organized. «مستطر»

8- The trial of people on the Day of Judgment is based on written documentation. «كل صغیر و كبیر مستطر»

9- The pious will be successful both materially, «فى جنّات و نهر», and spiritually. «فى مقعد صدق عند ملیك مقتدر»

10- Allah is Omnipotent both in His wrath, «اخذ عزیز مقتدر», and in His mercy. «فى مقعد صدق عند ملیك مقتدر»

11- Paradise is entirely truthfulness (sincerity), and not an iota of deceit finds its way into it. «مقعد صدق»
